slices.title: Inbound mail webhook creates an automation run
SharePoint document sync surfaces in the files list
slices.blockerSummary: Graph delta token rejected by sandbox tenant; video honestly shows the failed sync. Logged in docs/decisions.md; build continued.

_field_notes.purpose: Single durable handover index. Survives context compaction (autothing re-reads it to resume) and is the human-readable handover. NOTE: the /goal evaluator reads only the transcript — a small fast model that does not open files — so autothing must ALSO print the global-gate verdict into the conversation. This file is for resume + the human; the printed line is what the evaluator confirms.
_field_notes.runId / runDir / host / profile: The run's identity. runDir is docs/autothing/runs/<runId>/; host + profile are echoed from RUN_LOG's RUN-START. profile (patch|feature|build) is the pipeline shape this run used.
_field_notes.runLog / runSpec / landing: Pointers to the run's versioned records: RUN_LOG.md (append-only journal at the repo root), RUN_SPEC.md (the spec-first artifact for runs > ~3 slices; absent for small work), and LANDING.md (the run's own audit packet, written when the terminal verdict prints). All small + versioned.
_field_notes.versioned vs unversioned (git hygiene, P: KEEP VERSIONED (cross-machine resume depends on exactly these): FLOW_PLAN.md, evidence-index.json, gate-status.json, RUN_LOG.md, RUN_SPEC.md, LANDING.md. IGNORED (evidence binaries + liveness state): **/*.cast, *.webm, *.mp4, *.png, screenshot dirs, status.json, owner.lock.json. Because the binaries are unversioned, each slice's video artifact carries videoBytes + videoSha256 here so the evidence stays auditable from the versioned index.
_field_notes.gatesConfig: The operator gate toggles for this run (all true = full-bar), including codexSliceReview (the conditional per-slice Codex pass), codexCheckpoint, deliberateRed, mutation, and askQuestions; plus the profile. Set from the --no-*/--gates/--only/--minimal/--ask-questions/--no-deliberate-red/--no-mutation/--no-codex-slice flags at Phase 0 and re-read on resume (a compacted session has lost the original args). NOTE (Part 8.1): the old same-model 'review' key is GONE — the per-slice review is now the single fresh-context adversarialReview. A gate whose flag is false is SKIPPED, recorded as {status:skipped, reason:operator-disabled}, printed as 'GATE <name>: skipped (operator-disabled)', excluded from the passed check, and rendered 'off' plus named in the verdict's gates-disabled:<list> token. Disabling a gate makes a 'passed' an explicit reduced-bar pass, never the silent full-bar one.
_field_notes.slices[].kind: ui | api | mixed. Drives kind-conditional gates: designAudit + walkthrough run for ui/mixed only; api slices keep asciinema evidence; per-slice adversarialTest runs for ui/mixed while api slices get one batched run-level pass.
_field_notes.slices[].videoBytes / .videoSha256 / .vi: Part 2.3 + Part 4. The evidence artifact's size + sha256 (auditable though the binary itself is gitignored); videoMode is 'evidence' under autothing (marketing standalone); evidence points at the evidence-mode evidence.json ([{beat, criterionId, timestamp, assert, result}]).
_field_notes.slices[].status: passed | failed | blocked | in_progress  (mirrors that slice's gate-status.json)
_field_notes.slices[].flagged: true = honest-failure run (video shows it not working). Counts as recorded evidence, not a pass.
_field_notes.globalGate.status: in_progress | passed | completed-with-blockers. 'passed' ONLY when every objective exit==0, the securityWall is clean, verifyApp/designAudit verdict=='clean', decorrelatedVerification.reviewAllApproved && decorrelatedVerification.pwTestAllPassed (every slice got a clean fresh-context review 'approve' and an independent-test 'pass'), everySliceVideoVerified==true, the builtinSecurityReview is clean/fixed, AND codexCheckpoint.status is 'clean' or 'issues-fixed'. If buildable work is exhausted but any slice is blocked, its video is not verified, or a security gate is 'issues-open', the terminal state is 'completed-with-blockers' (enumerated in the handover) — never reported as 'passed'.
_field_notes.globalGate.securityWall: the run-level roll-up of the universal deterministic security wall (gitleaks/semgrep/dependency-audit, Part 12.5). Per-slice results live in gate-status.securityWall; this is the whole-repo confirmation.
_field_notes.globalGate.decorrelatedVerification: reviewAllApproved == every slice's fresh-context Anthropic review (autothing-adversarial-review — now THE per-slice review, Part 8.1) ended in a clean 'approve'. pwTestAllPassed == every slice's independent Anthropic test pass (autothing-adversarial-test) returned 'pass' (api slices via the batched run-level pass). Per-slice decorrelation is by CONTEXT, not vendor; a CONDITIONAL cross-model Codex pass ALSO runs per slice for profile=build / boundary-feature (see globalGate.codexSliceReview + gate-status.codexSliceReview).
_field_notes.globalGate.codexSliceReview: Roll-up of the CONDITIONAL per-slice cross-model Codex adversarial pass (gate-status.codexSliceReview; reinstated from run 20260706 evidence). ran = slices that actually ran it (build always; feature only security-boundary; never patch), approved = clean verdicts, degraded = codex-unavailable (quota/auth death — recorded, notified, run continues, never faked). The terminal verdict token is codexSlice:<approved>/<ran|off>. For 'passed', every slice that RAN it must be approved; a degraded slot does not block but is named in LANDING. Distinct from globalGate.codexCheckpoint (the run-level checkpoint, unchanged). All codex delegate (bridge) calls serialize; auth via API KEY not ChatGPT sign-in.
_field_notes.globalGate.mutation: Part 9.2 — mutation score on changed modules (Stryker or equivalent), optional-but-default for runs >= 3 slices (--no-mutation to disable). A ratchet: never decreases. Answers 'do the committed tests actually detect bugs?' with math and zero tokens.
_field_notes.globalGate.deliberateRed: Part 5.5 — default ON for runs >= 3 slices (--no-deliberate-red to disable). Once per run, plant one violation per structural gate (a boundary-breaking import, a fake secret), show the gate genuinely fail, revert, log the evidence. gatesProven lists which gates were seen red.
_field_notes.everySliceVideoVerified: true ONLY when every non-skipped ui/mixed slice has gates.video.status == 'verified'. A 'failed-but-unblocking' or 'evidence-degraded' video does NOT count as verified — it forces 'completed-with-blockers', matching the /goal condition 'a self-verified video exists for every slice'.
_field_notes.globalGate.builtinSecurityReview: Part 12.6 — Claude Code's built-in security review, run ONCE in the final phase BEFORE the codex checkpoint (so the cross-model pass adversarially checks what the built-in review already cleaned — two decorrelated passes, not two overlapping ones). Profiles: build always; feature when security-boundary surfaces were touched; patch never un-escalated. status: clean | issues-fixed | issues-open.
_field_notes.globalGate.codexCheckpoint: the ONE genuine cross-model check in the pipeline (autothing-codex-checkpoint), run ONCE in the final phase AFTER the built-in security review, not per slice. status: clean | issues-fixed | issues-open | skipped. scopes[]: one entry per scoped codex delegate (bridge) invocation ({scope, verdict, by, effort, at, report} or {scope, verdict:'skipped', reason}). issues-open (a real, agreed violation surviving its 2-recheck ceiling) forces 'completed-with-blockers'. Codex calls are SERIAL (concurrent codex delegate (bridge) revokes the shared OAuth token) — see autothing-codex-checkpoint/references/codex-checkpoint.md.
_field_notes.globalGate.modelFallbacks: count of times a gate's pinned model was replaced this run (limit/credit/capacity/classifier-redirect, Part 3.1). When >0, the terminal verdict carries a model-fallbacks:<n> token and each replacement is a DECISION entry in RUN_LOG naming which model replaced which and why. A degraded gate is recorded degraded, never presented as the pinned bar.
